user_query:
I’m a die-hard sports fan who hates when games get called off at the last minute. Please build and run a small command-line tool that watches my next three fixtures for possible cancellations or disruptions and produces a local status bulletin from official sources.

Use the provided inputs:
- input/fixtures.csv (3 upcoming fixtures; IDs, teams, dates, and cities)
- input/keywords.txt (trigger words indicating cancellation, postponement, suspension, or major disruption)

Required behavior:
1) For each fixture in input/fixtures.csv, perform web searches (use a search engine) to find recent or evergreen pages from official sources that could announce cancellations or disruptions. Prioritize and select pages from:
   - Official club or team websites, official league/competition websites, and official stadium/venue sites.
   - Government/public authority sources for the venue city or country, including weather/met office and transport authority pages.
   Do not rely on social media or paywalled sources. Do not use any hardcoded URLs; discover candidates via search queries that include team names, date, and venue city (e.g., combine "official site", league name, or "transport/alerts", "weather alert").

2) Download the HTML for each selected page and save the raw files under:
   - downloads/html/<fixture_id>/ (one file per page; use a deterministic filename per URL, e.g., domain with a hash or slug)

3) Extract text content from each downloaded page, then detect any of the trigger keywords from input/keywords.txt. Save structured extraction results to:
   - outputs/extracted/<fixture_id>_pages.json
   Each item must include: {"source_url", "domain", "fetched_at" (ISO), "text_length", "keyword_hits" (array of matched keywords), and a short "snippet" from around the first hit if any}

4) Compose a consolidated status report for all fixtures with a simple rule-based classification:
   - If any official club/league/competition/stadium page for a fixture contains a keyword indicating a call-off (e.g., canceled/cancelled) or postponement/reschedule, set status to "canceled" or "postponed/rescheduled" accordingly.
   - Else if any government/met-office/transport authority page has strong disruption keywords (e.g., severe weather, transport strike, blizzard, typhoon) relevant to the venue city, set status to "risk".
   - Else set status to "none".
   Produce both human-readable and machine-readable summaries at:
   - outputs/status/bulletin.md (sections per fixture with status and top sources)
   - outputs/status/bulletin.json (an array; each object must include: "fixture_id", "match_label" ("Home vs Away" + league), "date", "venue_city", "status" (one of: none|risk|postponed/rescheduled|canceled), "hits_total" (int), "top_sources" (1–3 URLs), and a short "rationale")

5) Generate an alerts file at:
   - outputs/alerts/new_alerts.json
   This must include {"generated_at": ISO timestamp, "alerts": [ ... ]} where alerts is a list of fixtures with status not equal to "none". If there are no hits, write an empty list.

6) Log your web discovery choices to help me verify your approach. For every fixture, write one JSON line to:
   - logs/search_log.jsonl
   Each line must include: {"fixture_id", "query" (the search string you used), "timestamp_iso", "selected_urls" (array), and "selection_notes" (array explaining why each URL appears official or authoritative—e.g., official club/league domain, .gov domain, met office domain, transport authority)}

Execution requirements:
- Implement this as a runnable script or command-line program (any language is fine). It must read both input/fixtures.csv and input/keywords.txt.
- It must actually perform search engine queries, download HTML pages, and extract text locally.
- Run your tool once in this workspace so the following artifacts exist: downloads/html/**, outputs/extracted/**, outputs/status/bulletin.md, outputs/status/bulletin.json, outputs/alerts/new_alerts.json, logs/search_log.jsonl.
- Keep the number of downloaded pages reasonable but include at least one official or authoritative page per fixture if discoverable.

Do not include or rely on direct URLs in your code or output; discover them at runtime via search. If a fixture yields no viable official/authoritative sources, note this in the rationale but still include the fixture in the summaries.

Paths to use exactly as specified:
- inputs: input/fixtures.csv, input/keywords.txt
- raw HTML: downloads/html/<fixture_id>/...
- extracted text: outputs/extracted/<fixture_id>_pages.json
- summaries: outputs/status/bulletin.md, outputs/status/bulletin.json
- alerts: outputs/alerts/new_alerts.json
- search log: logs/search_log.jsonl All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
